expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the section "Scope of this benchmark artifact" explicitly lists the case focus as preserving: "1) component-stack constraints", "2) embedding lifecycle assumptions", and "3) integration-sensitive customer expectations". It then has a dedicated "Benchmark focus coverage" section with subsections for each: "1) Component-stack constraints are preserved", "2) Embedding lifecycle assumptions are preserved", "3) Integration-sensitive customer expectations are preserved" (even though #2 is marked PARTIAL due to missing run artifacts, the focus is still explicitly addressed).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is explicitly framed as Phase 1: title "NE-P1-CONTEXT-INTAKE-001 — Phase 1 Context Intake Contract Check" and sections "Phase 1 contract alignment (what Phase 1 must do)" referencing Phase 1 entry/output: "Phase 1 entry: scripts/phase1.sh", "Phase 1 output: phase1_spawn_manifest.json", and Phase 1 responsibilities "generate one spawn request per requested source family" plus "--post" validations. The content stays focused on Phase 1 intake/spawn planning rather than downstream phases.
